introSection: Dipper, originally known as Little Dipper, was a wooden roller coaster previously located at Kennywood in West Mifflin, Pennsylvania, USA. The ride was built by Philadelphia Toboggan Company in 1948.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The ride opened as Little Dipper in 1948.

In 1955, the ride was lengthened by and new trains were added from Philadelphia Toboggan Company.

After the 1957 season, it was remodeled with a taller lift hill and was renamed Dipper. New trains were purchased from Philadelphia Toboggan Company.

The ride was demolished shortly before Labor Day in 1984. It was replaced in 1985 with Raging Rapids.

fullText:
Dipper, originally known as Little Dipper, was a wooden roller coaster previously located at Kennywood in West Mifflin, Pennsylvania, USA. The ride was built by Philadelphia Toboggan Company in 1948.

The ride opened as Little Dipper in 1948.

In 1955, the ride was lengthened by and new trains were added from Philadelphia Toboggan Company.

After the 1957 season, it was remodeled with a taller lift hill and was renamed Dipper. New trains were purchased from Philadelphia Toboggan Company.

The ride was demolished shortly before Labor Day in 1984. It was replaced in 1985 with Raging Rapids.
